chapter: Chapter: Changes around us : Physical and Chemical

questions.question: What is a physical change?
questions.answer: A change in which only the physical properties of a substance change and no new substance is formed.

questions.question: What is a chemical change?
questions.answer: A change in which one or more new substances are formed.

questions.question: Give one example of a physical change.
Give one example of a chemical change.
Is the tearing of paper a physical or chemical change?
questions.answer: Tearing of paper is a physical change because no new substance is formed.

questions.question: What is rusting?
questions.answer: The formation of a brown flaky substance on iron when exposed to moist air.

questions.question: What two things are necessary for rusting to occur?
What is crystallization?
questions.answer: The process of obtaining large crystals of a pure substance from its solution.

questions.question: What is galvanization?
questions.answer: The process of depositing a layer of zinc on iron to prevent rusting.

questions.question: Is boiling of water a physical or chemical change?
What color is Magnesium Oxide?
What happens when magnesium ribbon is burnt in air?
questions.answer: It burns with a brilliant white light and forms a powdery ash called magnesium oxide.

questions.question: State one way to prevent rusting besides galvanization.
questions.answer: Painting or greasing the iron surface.

questions.question: Is the digestion of food a physical or chemical change?
What is the color of Copper Sulphate solution?
What happens to the color of copper sulphate solution when an iron nail is placed in it?
questions.answer: The blue color changes to green.

questions.question: Write the chemical name of rust.
Is making a salt solution a physical or chemical change?
What gas is produced when baking soda reacts with vinegar?
What is the test for carbon dioxide gas?
Is photosynthesis a physical or chemical change?
What is stainless steel made of?
questions.answer: It is made by mixing iron with carbon and metals like chromium, nickel, and manganese.

questions.question: Is stretching a rubber band a physical or chemical change?
Why is crystallization considered a physical change?
questions.answer: Because no new substance is formed; only the size and shape of the crystals change.

questions.question: Which type of change is generally reversible?
